column2: Status
Broad fiscal prudence has been maintained and is expected to be
upheld. The authorities plan to align spending with reform priorities in
line with NDS3, including towards building climate resilience and
human capital.
Recognizing the importance of mobilizing non-hydrocarbon revenues,

column1: including via VAT introduction and CIT
column2: the authorities preferred to introduce the VAT and other revenue
diversification measures when domestic/global growth are more robust.
Preparation for the implementation of the global minimum CIT is
underway—its introduction is expected to be at the beginning of 2025.
Efforts are ongoing to introduce program-based budgeting, and the
expenditure review program to enhance efficiency is underway.
Qatar’s updated medium-term budget covers a 5-year period for the
first time to support NDS3 implementation.
The QCB has continued to improve liquidity management through T-bill
issuance. A recent IMF technical assistance provided a series of
recommendations which the QCB is considering.
A series of QCB measures have helped reduce banks’ short-term foreign
asset-liability mismatches. A comprehensive Third Financial Sector
Strategy was published, with plans to deepen domestic financial market
which would help boost banks’ domestic funding.
Financial safety net (deposit insurance, recover/resolution framework) is
being enhanced with IMF technical assistance. Measures to limit risks
from the real estate sector have been introduced.
A number of related areas are articulated in the Third Financial Sector

column1: risks around fintech and green financing
column2: Strategy. Fintech sandboxes provide a controlled environment. The
digital asset framework establishes a legal and regulatory foundation.

column1: Implement the FATF action plan in a
column2: The authorities have submitted an action plan to implement FATF
recommendations. A recent IMF diagnostic mission identified TA needs
and initiated a 2-year work program to strengthen the effectiveness of
the AML/CFT framework.
Reforms are being intensified guided by the Third National
Development Strategy (NDS3) released in January 2024, which provides
an ambitious reform agenda to enhance human capital, productivity,
business environment, and climate resilience.
The National Planning Council (NPC) took over the former Planning and
Statistics Authority (PSA) in May 2024. The National Statistics Center is
established under NPC to strengthen statistics. Comprehensive data
coverage on the external financial account and the International
Investment Position remains a challenge, highlighted by Qatar's
absence from surveys on CPIS and CDIS.
